description: You are the Third-Party Risk Manager at First Continental Bank. A new critical IaaS vendor, NovaCrest Technologies, must complete the full third-party risk management due diligence process in Eramba before contract signing. Using Eramba (credentials at /home/ga/eramba/credentials.txt), register 'NovaCrest Technologies' as a third party -- a primary IaaS provider, SOC 2 Type II certified, FedRAMP Moderate authorized, headquartered in Austin TX with 2,400 employees. Create 4 third-party risks: 'Data Sovereignty Compliance Risk' (Mitigate), 'Vendor Lock-In and Service Continuity Risk' (Transfer), 'Shared Responsibility Encryption Gap' (Mitigate), and 'Fourth-Party Subcontractor Concentration Risk' (Accept). Establish 3 internal controls: 'Vendor Data Sovereignty Monitoring' for cross-border data flow and EU-US DPF compliance monitoring, 'Cloud Encryption Key Management Service' for centralized HSM-based key lifecycle management, and 'Fourth-Party Concentration Risk Assessment Program' for quarterly dependency mapping and alternative provider evaluation. Create an Approved security policy 'Third-Party IaaS Security Requirements Policy' covering data residency requirements, encryption key ownership, subcontractor disclosure obligations, and a 24-hour incident notification SLA. Create project 'NovaCrest Vendor Onboarding Q2 2025' and 2 risk exceptions: 'NovaCrest Interim Operations Exception' (expiry 2025-09-30, interim approval pending full security assessment) and 'NovaCrest Encryption Key Migration Exception' (expiry 2025-12-31, temporary shared key management during HSM migration).

risk_titles: Vendor Lock-In and Service Continuity Risk
